result.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: 1. Adjacency Representation: Represent the city map as a graph where each color is a node and edges represent required adjacencies. Use an adjacency list for colors and a 2D grid with 4-connectivity (up, down, left, right) for the 50x50 layout. 2. Implementation Strategy: Use Disjoint Set Union (DSU) to manage and verify connectivity constraints during placement. Use a BFS-based flood fill algorithm to define the boundaries of each color cluster to ensure they are contiguous. 3. Placement Heuristics: Sort color clusters by a priority metric of (degree * area), where degree is the number of required adjacencies and area is the number of cells required for that color. Prioritize placing high-degree, large-area clusters in the center or corners to minimize fragmentation. 4. Optimization via Local Search: Employ Simulated Annealing to maximize empty cells (E). Define moves such as: (a) Cluster-shifting: Moving a contiguous color block to a new valid position; (b) Edge-swapping: Swapping adjacent cells of different colors if they satisfy adjacency constraints; and (c) Compaction: Attempting to shrink the bounding box of a cluster to free up adjacent cells. 
JUSTIFICATION: This approach combines graph theory (DSU/Adjacency lists) with spatial optimization (Simulated Annealing/Flood Fill) to satisfy topological constraints while maximizing the objective function through iterative compaction.
